name: MatchedEvents
definition: Count of events that matched at least one EventBridge rule. Best traffic proxy for routing work entering the rule engine; it does not measure all published events.
variants: or rules no longer match incoming events
citations: Prefer rule or source groupings; `context.rulename` is bounded but still better in top-lists than dense multi-line charts

name: TriggeredRules
definition: Count of rules that triggered from matched events. Higher than MatchedEvents usually means multi-rule fanout rather than more producer traffic.
absence_means: No rules are triggering because the namespace is idle or rule patterns no longer match
cardinality_footguns: Rule names can grow if teams create many narrow rules so default to top-lists before dense timeseries

name: InvocationsCreated
definition: Count of target invocations created by EventBridge. Primary fanout metric because one matched event can create multiple target deliveries.
absence_means: No target work is being created because no rules are firing or the namespace is idle
cardinality_footguns: This metric has no `context.rulename` in Tsuga so keep grouping at source or Region level

name: InvocationAttempts
definition: Count of target invocation attempts including retries. Rising faster than InvocationsCreated usually indicates downstream retry pressure or repeated delivery failures.
absence_means: No delivery attempts are happening because no target work is being created or the namespace is idle
cardinality_footguns: Attempts are retry-inflated so do not compare directly to business event counts

name: SuccessfulInvocationAttempts
definition: Count of successful target delivery attempts. Best success numerator for delivery quality in the current namespace.
absence_means: No successful deliveries are occurring because traffic is idle or delivery attempts are failing
cardinality_footguns: Use alongside InvocationAttempts and FailedInvocations to separate idle from broken

name: Invocations
definition: Count of target invocations recorded by EventBridge. Useful denominator for durable failure share and overall target work completed.
absence_means: No completed invocation work is being recorded because the namespace is idle or deliveries are stalling
cardinality_footguns: Interpret with care because retries live in InvocationAttempts not Invocations

name: FailedInvocations
definition: Count of failed target invocations after EventBridge could not deliver successfully. Strongest durable failure metric in the current namespace.
absence_means: No durable target failures were recorded or the namespace is idle
cardinality_footguns: Use top-lists instead of many-line timeseries when many rules exist

name: IngestiontoInvocationStartLatency
definition: Latency from event ingestion until EventBridge starts target invocation. Best signal for queueing or throttle delay before the first delivery attempt.
absence_means: No successful routing work reached the start point or the metric is stale
cardinality_footguns: Tsuga reports summary+cumulative with avg|max|min|sum capabilities; avoid percentiles unless Stage 3 explicitly re-validates them

name: IngestiontoInvocationCompleteLatency
definition: Latency from event ingestion until an invocation attempt completes. Useful midpoint to separate start delay from end-to-end success delay.
absence_means: No completed attempts were recorded or the metric is stale
cardinality_footguns: Use with start and success latency rather than alone

name: IngestiontoInvocationSuccessLatency
definition: Latency from event ingestion until EventBridge records a successful delivery. Best end-to-end latency signal for user-visible delivery health.
absence_means: No successful deliveries completed or the metric is stale
cardinality_footguns: Use rule-level top lists rather than noisy target splits because no target id field is present

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: EventBridge cost is driven mainly by published custom events, matched rules, and target invocations, but the current `aws_events_*` namespace does not expose a direct billing metric. Use `aws_events_matched_events` and `aws_events_invocations_created` as operational cost proxies and use AWS Cost Explorer for actual spend.
optional_context_fields: No direct cost metric in this namespace
